experiment: x86 software prefetch in q8_0_packed_rows4_dot (CAMELID_X86_PREFETCH), CPU Q8 decode
host: i7-11800H (16T, Tiger Lake AVX-512) / Win11 / 15.7 GiB DDR4

parity.evidence: output_token_ids SHA-256 == 297fc9f8dcc1 for ALL iterations, flag OFF and flag ON. A memory hint cannot change the decoded values — Class A by construction, confirmed empirically.

verdict: NULL (marginally slower, within run-to-run noise: -0.8% median). x86 software prefetch does NOT help CPU Q8 decode on this box.
why: CPU decode is DRAM-bandwidth-bound (~6.4 tok/s x 3.18 GiB = ~20 GB/s achieved, ~40% of the ~51 GB/s 2-channel DDR4 peak). The hardware prefetcher already covers the sequential weight stream; software prefetch adds instruction overhead without unblocking the bandwidth ceiling. Consistent with the GAIT campaign's prefetch null.
decision: Flag CAMELID_X86_PREFETCH stays DEFAULT-OFF (regression guard); NOT promoted to default. Re-measure if a non-bandwidth-bound host or the Phase-2 K-quant kernels change the access pattern.
huge_pages_status: DEFERRED — see KQUANT_RECON.md Phase 3 appendix. The MADV_HUGEPAGE hint lives in the #[cfg(unix)] mmap path, which does not compile/run on this Windows box (Windows uses the memmap2 path with no madvise). Not shipped here to honor 'do not ship code unverifiable on the host you have'.
